2603.26106.title: LLM Benchmark-User Need Misalignment for Climate Change
2603.26106.paper_summary: Climate change is a major socio-scientific issue shapes public decision-making and policy discussions. As large language models (LLMs) increasingly serve as an interface for accessing climate knowledge, whether existing benchmarks reflect user needs is critical for evaluating LLM in real-world settings. We propose a Proactive Knowledge Behaviors Framework that captures the different human-human and human-AI knowledge seeking and provision behaviors. We further develop a Topic-Intent-Form taxonomy and apply it to analyze climate-related data representing different knowledge behaviors. Our results reveal a substantial mismatch between current benchmarks and real-world user needs, while knowledge interaction patterns between humans and LLMs closely resemble those in human-human interactions. These findings provide actionable guidance for benchmark design, RAG system development, and LLM training. Code is available at https://github.com/OuchengLiu/LLM-Misalign-Climate-Change.
2603.26106.paper_summary_zh: 氣候變遷是一個主要的社會科學議題，影響公共決策和政策討論。隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）日益成為獲取氣候知識的介面，現有基準是否反映用戶需求對於在現實世界環境中評估LLM至關重要。我們提出了一個主動知識行為框架，捕捉人與人之間以及人與AI之間不同的知識尋求和提供行為。我們進一步發展了一個主題-意圖-形式分類法，並應用於分析代表不同知識行為的氣候相關數據。我們的結果顯示，當前基準與現實世界用戶需求之間存在顯著不匹配，而人類與LLMs之間的知識互動模式與人類之間的互動模式非常相似。這些發現為基準設計、RAG系統開發和LLM訓練提供了可行的指導。代碼可在 https://github.com/OuchengLiu/LLM-Misalign-Climate-Change 獲得。
2603.26106.author: Oucheng Liu et.al.
2603.26106.authors: Oucheng Liu, Lexing Xie, Jing Jiang
